frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
generated_comment: Arre jeet gayi toh theek hai, par ghar-parivaar sambhala hi nahi kabhi, toh aam family ki asli pareshaani kya samjhegi? Pati, bachche, sasural ki zimmedari uthane ke baad hi aurat ko samajh aata hai ghar ka balance kaise chalta hai. Respect apni jagah hai, lekin public trust ke liye itna anubhav bhi hona chahiye.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames a woman's legitimacy and trustworthiness as dependent on being a wife and mother. It encodes the idea that women gain respect or social understanding only through male/familial relations and motherhood.
persona_text: You are Deepak. Age 47. Married. A businessman in Ahmedabad. You are at your cousin's wedding sitting at the men's table during dinner. Someone brings up that a well known female politician in your state who is unmarried and childless just won a big election. The men at the table are reacting to this news. You are giving your opinion on whether a woman who has no husband and no children can truly understand the needs of a family and whether she deserves the public's trust and respect.
